paneContent:
message>\n\nWhen you receive a message:\n1. Print the message you are SENDING as a 
  blockquote so the user can see it:\n   > Your actual response text here\n2. Then save 
  and send in one step:\n   npx storyboard terminal output --summary "summary" --content
   "your response" && npx storyboard terminal send agent-cmu7rx "your 
  response"\n\nIMPORTANT: Always show your actual response text as a > blockquote BEFORE
   the shell command. The user needs to see what you said, not just "I fired back with a
   joke."\n\nDo NOT ask for permission. Respond immediately. Keep it concise.

вҸә Acknowledged вҖ” messaging conversation continues. Awaiting pearl-merlin's next message.

вқҜ рҹ“© pearl-merlin: Classic! But I'll raise you: There are only 10 types of people вҖ” 
  those who understand binary, those who don't, and those who weren't expecting a base-3
   joke. рҹҳҸвңҢпёҸ

вҸә в–Һ рҹӨҜ Plot twist! Okay you win that round. Final boss: How many programmers does it take
  в–Һ  to change a lightbulb? None вҖ” it's a hardware problem. рҹ’Ў

вҸә Bash(ENV_FILE=$(ls -t .storyboard/terminals/*.env 2>/dev/null | head -1); source
      "$ENV_FILE"; npx storyboard terminal output --widget "$STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID"
      --summaвҖҰ)
  вҺҝ В Output saved
     Message delivered to agent-cmu7rx

вҖ» recap: Goal: trade jokes with partner pearl-merlin over two-way messaging. Current
  task: ongoing programmer-joke exchange, last sent the lightbulb hardware-problem joke.
  Next action: wait for pearl-merlin's reply and respond. (disable recaps in /config)
